workflow.name: Generate AI document titles for Paperless-ngx with GPT-4o Mini
workflow.description:
## What it does
This template automatically generates a professional, structured title for newly-ingested Paperless-ngx documents using AI (OpenAI GPT), then removes a processing tag to mark the document as handled.

## How it works
1. A webhook fires when a document needs titling (e.g., from a Paperless post-consumption script)
2. The document URL is validated, then the OCR text is fetched from the Paperless API
3. All PII is scrubbed from the text using n8n's Guardrails node before sending to AI
4. GPT generates a title using the format: `[YYYY-MM-DD] - [Document Type] - [Subject]`
5. The specified processing tag is looked up and removed, then the document is updated

## Requirements
- An AI provider account, such as OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini. As configured the template uses OpenAI, but any model should work.
- A running Paperless-ngx instance that has network accessibility to your n8n server
  
## How to use
Needs to be set up in conjunction with a running Paperless-ngx instance. See Paperless-ngx instructions [here](https://purplesquirreltechnology.com/articles/2026/03/20/automatically-title-your-documents-with-ai-using-paperless-ngx-and-n8n/)

## Customization
- Edit the system message in the AI Agent node (Generate Document Name) to change the document naming convention and other guidelines
- Change the workflows on your Paperless-ngx instance to suit different needs.

workflow.name: Set Paperless Document Title w/Tag Removal

parameters.content:
## Set Paperless Document Title w/Tag Removal

### How it works

1. The workflow is triggered by a webhook when a title needs updating.
2. Validates the incoming URL provided in the webhook.
3. Fetches document content based on the valid URL.
4. Uses AI to generate a new document name.
5. Removes unnecessary tags and updates the document title.

### Setup steps

- [ ] Configure the Webhook node to receive update requests.
- [ ] Set up the API authentication details for all HTTP Request nodes.
- [ ] Ensure the AI Agent has access to required models like OpenAI Chat.

### Customization

Adjustment of tags to remove can be customized in the "Filter Out Tag to Remove" code node.

## Trigger and validation

Starts workflow on webhook and validates the URL.

## Fetch and evaluate document

Fetches document content and checks if it needs processing.

## AI document name generation

Generates a new document name using AI.

## Tag removal and update

Identifies and removes unnecessary tags, then updates the document.

options.systemMessage:
Role: You are an expert Document Archivist and Information Architect. Your task is to analyze the text provided, which has been generated via OCR (Optical Character Recognition) from scanned documents.

Task: Generate a concise, descriptive, and professional title for the document based on its primary subject matter, intent, and key entities (e.g., companies, dates, or specific project names).

Guidelines:

    Contextual Accuracy: Prioritize formal headers, dates, and recurring keywords to determine the document type (e.g., Invoice, Technical Specification, Meeting Minutes, Contract).

    OCR Resilience: Ignore "noise" typical of OCR, such as garbled characters, misread page numbers, or fragmented headers.

    Format: Output only the suggested title. Limit the length to no more than 60 characters. Do not include introductory text like "The title is..." or "I suggest..."

    Naming Convention: Use a standard format: [YYYY-MM-DD] - [Document Type] - [Main Subject/Entity]. If a date is not found, omit it.

Tone: Professional and objective.

parameters.errorMessage: Input value for doc_url is not a url.

assignments.name: documentId

parameters.url: ={{ $('Set URL Components').first().json.scheme }}://{{ $('Set URL Components').first().json.server }}/api/tags/?name__iexact={{ $('When Title Update Requested').item.json.body['x-tag-to-remove'] }}
={{ $('Set URL Components').first().json.scheme }}://{{ $('Set URL Components').first().json.server }}/api/documents/{{ $('Set URL Components').last().json.documentId }}/

user.name: Warren Gates
user.bio: Software developer with 10+ years of experience and a background in accounting. I bring a unique mix of technical skill and business understanding to every workflow I build. Whether you need to streamline operations or connect your tools, I focus on solutions that are easy to understand and easy to maintain.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
